data.title: Godavari Biorefineries IPO subscribed 27% on day 1, retail quota booked 48%
data.description:
Godavari Biorefineries IPO

Godavari Biorefineries' Rs 555-crore initial public offering had a muted start on October 23, the first day of bidding. The issue was subscribed 27 percent with investors buying 30.48 lakh equity shares against offer size of 1.12 crore shares.

The IPO is a mix of fresh issue of Rs 325 crore, and an offer-for-sale of 65.26 lakh shares worth Rs 229.75 crore by existing shareholders including promoters. The price band for the issue, which closes on October 25, has been fixed at Rs 334-352 per share.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

As per the subscription data with the exchanges, retail investors bid 48 percent shares of their reservation, and non-institutional investors bought 12 percent shares of their portion set aside for them. Qualified institutional buyers also started putting in bids for the issue, picking up 0.2 percent shares of their allotted quota.

On October 22, the ethanol producer mobilised Rs 166.42 crore out of the total issue size, from 15 anchor investors at the upper price band. Key investors that participated in the anchor book on Tuesday were ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund, HDFC Mutual Fund, Whiteoak Capital Mutual Fund, 360 ONE Equity Opportunity Fund, Goldman Sachs, Societe Generale and Vikas Khemani-backed Carnelian Structural Shift Fund.

Also read: Managed office space firm IndiQube to file papers for Rs 800-crore IPO in November

Investor Mandala Capital AG is going to exit Godavari Biorefineries by selling its entire shareholding of 49.26 lakh shares in the offer-for-sale.

The Mumbai-based company makes bio-based chemicals, sugar, ethanol and power, having a bio-refinery capacity of 570KLPD for manufacturing ethanol, which supplies products to several industries such as food, beverages, pharmaceuticals, flavours & fragrances, power, fuel, personal care and cosmetics.

It will repay its debt of Rs 240 crore through fresh issue proceeds. And the remainder fresh issue money will be used for general corporate purposes.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Sona Comstar to acquire railway equipment division of Escorts Kubota for Rs 1,600 crore; Q2 net profit jumps 15% YoY
data.description:
The proposed acquisition is expected to be earnings accretive for Sona Comstar right from the first year and offers significant growth opportunities, the company said in an exchange filing.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Sona BLW Precision Forgings Ltd. has entered into an agreement to acquire the Railway Equipment Division of Escorts Kubota for Rs 1,600 crore.

The proposed acquisition is expected to be earnings accretive for Sona Comstar right from the first year and offers significant growth opportunities, the company said in an exchange filing.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Railway Equipment Division of Escorts Kubota is a leading supplier of critical components for Railways, such as brakes and suspension systems for various rolling stocks. It reported revenue of about Rs 950 crore in FY24.

Commenting on the proposed acquisition, Vivek Vikram Singh, MD & Group CEO of Sona Comstar, said: "Once completed, the ‘Railway Equipment Division’ acquisition will enhance our clean mobility product offerings by adding a market-leading railway components business."

"The railway industry presents long-term growth opportunities, and with the ‘Railway Equipment Division’ business, we see significant potential to broaden our product range by incorporating advanced technology and engineered products," he added.

The company on Wednesday also announced its September quarter results with net profit jumping 15.7 percent at Rs 143.6 Ccrore against Rs 124.1 crore on-year.

data.title: Hindustan Unilever Q2 net profit falls to Rs 2,591 crore, misses estimates; Rs 29 per share dividend declared
data.description:
Hindustan Unilever Q2 FY25 Results

Hindustan Unilever Ltd's Q2 FY25 consolidate net profit declined to Rs 2,591 crore from Rs 2,668 crore in the same quarter a year ago, falling more than Street expectations. A Moneycontrol poll of seven brokerages showed that HUL's July-September net profit was expected to fall half a percent to Rs 2,654 crore from a year ago.

The FMCG giant's second quarter consolidated revenue expanded on-year to Rs 16,145 crore, against the Moneycontrol poll prediction of Rs 15,694 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Hindustan Unilever's Q2 results were expected to be marred by high commodity inflation in certain segments, along with muted demand. Ahead of the results, brokerages expected HUL's margins to shrink due to higher advertisement spends and increased royalty to parent Unilever Ltd.

To be updated
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Technical View: Bears may further tighten grip if Nifty breaks 24,350, Bank Nifty defends 51,100
data.description:
Market Trend

The Nifty 50 sustained its downtrend for the third consecutive session but managed to defend the 24,400 mark on a closing basis due to buying interest at lower levels on October 23, a day before the weekly F&O expiry scheduled for tomorrow. If the index can maintain the 24,350-24,400 zone in the upcoming sessions, a bounce towards 24,600-24,700 is likely. However, failure to hold this range could drive the index down towards the 24,000 key support area, according to experts.

The Nifty 50 opened lower at 24,378, which was also the day’s low, but gained some strength to hit a day's high near 24,600 in late morning trades. However, the index could not sustain those gains due to selling pressure in the last couple of hours of trading and ultimately finished at 24,436, down 37 points. It formed a small bullish candlestick pattern with a long upper shadow, resembling a bullish Inverted Hammer pattern on the daily charts (though not a classical one).

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Thus, a sustainable move above the high of this pattern at 24,605 could confirm the positive pattern for the short term, according to Nagaraj Shetti, Senior Technical Research Analyst at HDFC Securities.

The negative chart pattern, characterized by lower tops and bottoms, remains intact as per both daily and weekly charts.

According to Shetti, the underlying trend of the Nifty remains weak. "A sustainable close only above the 24,650-24,700 levels could confirm an upside bounce. However, a slide below 24,350 is likely to drag the Nifty down to the 24,000 mark in the near term," he stated.

The weekly options data indicated that the index is likely to remain within the broader range of 24,000-25,000 in the short term.

On the Call side, the 25,000 strike holds the maximum open interest, followed by the 24,800 and 24,700 strikes, with maximum writing at the 25,000 strike, and then at the 24,600 and 24,500 strikes. On the Put front, maximum open interest was seen at the 24,000 strike, followed by the 24,200 and 24,400 strikes, with maximum writing at the 24,200 strike, and then at the 24,400 and 23,700 strikes.

Bank Nifty

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Bank Nifty opened on a negative note and drifted lower towards the 51,100 zone in the initial hour of the session. However, some recovery was seen from lower levels, but it failed to hold near the 51,500 area and remained consolidative in a narrow range of 200 points between the 51,150 and 51,350 zones in the latter part of the day. The index fell 18 points to close at 51,239 and formed a bullish Inverted Hammer pattern (not a classical one).

"Until the banking index holds below the 51,555 zone, weakness could be seen towards 51,000 and then 50,750 levels. On the upside, resistance is seen at 51,555 and then 51,750 levels," stated Chandan Taparia, Senior Vice President | Analyst-Derivatives at Motilal Oswal Financial Services.

Volatility maintained an upward bias, making the trend more favourable for bears. The India VIX, the fear index, increased by 1.58 percent to 14.62, up from 14.4 levels, hovering above all key moving averages, which is a negative sign for bulls.

data.title: Hindustan Unilever decides to separate its ice cream business
data.description:
Hindustan Unilever board decides to separate its ice cream business

live

  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Hindustan Unilever Ltd (HUL) on October 23 said its board announced its decision to separate the ice cream business.

"The board’s decision to separate is in line with the recommendation made by the Independent Committee following a comprehensive review of the business," said HUL in a stock exchange filing.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"This portfolio restructuring will enable HUL to sharpen focus on the core business and further strengthen its play in trending demand spaces such as Beauty, Foods, Health and Wellbeing," the FMCG major added.

The decision follows the announcement, earlier this year, by the company's parent entity, Unilever PLC, about its intention to separate its global ice cream business across jurisdictions.

(This is a developing story and will be updated soon.)

data.title: Equities taking money away from bank deposits is a misnomer, says Rashesh Shah of Edelweiss
data.description:
Rashesh Shah explained that that the cycle of money within the financial system shows how interlinked these sectors are

The belief that if an investor is investing in IPOs, it will take away from bank deposits is a “misnomer”, says Rashesh Shah, Co-founder, Edelweiss Group.

“It is a complete misnomer that because I am investing in an IPO, I am taking money away from deposits. That same money I invested in the IPO is getting deposited back in the bank,” said the market veteran while speaking during a panel discussion at the National Stock Exchange to commemorate Moneycontrol Pro reaching 1 million subscribers.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Shah explained that that the cycle of money within the financial system shows how interlinked these sectors are. “When my company gives me a salary, if I take that salary and invest in a mutual fund, it is again going back in the banks only,” he explained. This, he explained, highlights that while funds may temporarily shift into equities, they eventually return to the banking system.

He also spoke about the Indian financial landscape. “We have almost $1 trillion of household savings and in the last few years, we have become capital self-sufficient,” he said adding that India is currently less reliant on foreign capital.

Shah added that while the colour of the deposits is undergoing a change, India has a lot of capital. “In fact, we have more capital than we can smartly invest and that's why… cost of capital is cheaper in India. The fact is we have more demand than supply of good quality equity paper. So idea is to address that, rather than say how we get more people to go back to bank deposits,” he explained.

He compared this evolution in India's financial landscape to the country’s self-sufficiency in agriculture. “About 30 years ago, India became self-sufficient in food. We have always felt we were not self-sufficient for capital,” he said.

Shah added that investors have become far more educated. "They understand how much they are earning after tax on a bank deposit versus what they earn in the market,” he said.

“Given all of that, I think the relative attractiveness of investing in a bank deposit is going down and the relative attractiveness of investing in the markets is going up,” he said while highlighting that it’s not a case of either or. “We need a banking system and a capital market system,” he said.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Shah also noted that banks would increasingly need to integrate capital market capabilities. “All over the world, banks are both banking and capital markets. In India, we have this artificial divide.” He believes that in the next eight to ten years, we will see this happening.

In July 2024, both SEBI and RBI, during different events, had raised concerns about household funds moving from banks to other investment avenues like equities, which in turn was impacting their liquidity.

In August 2024, CNBC-TV18 had reported that Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman is set to conduct a thorough review of the performance of Public Sector Banks (PSBs) and Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) to assess critical financial indicators such as deposit growth, credit-deposit (CD) ratios, and asset quality.

In its September 2024 India strategy report, Motilal Oswal Financial Services stated that systemic credit growth for banks has declined, mainly driven by slower deposit growth and an elevated CD ratio. “Deposit growth has hovered in a narrow range of 11-13% over the past 14 months and is up 2.8% YTD,” stated the report.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: OBSC Perfection subscribed 1.29x on day 2 so far, issue plans to raise Rs 66 crore
data.description:
The company proposes to utilize the net proceeds from the issue towards funding the capital expenditure requirements, working capital requirements and gerneral corporate purposes.

OBSC Perfection was subscribed nearly 1.29 times on day 2 till 4 pm. The Rs 66.02 crore issue has a lot size of 1,200 shares.

The company has fixed a price band of Rs 95-100 with a minimum investment of Rs 1,14,000 per lot and a maximum investment of Rs 1,20,000 per lot.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The issue will conclude for subscription on October 24 and the shares of the precision metal components manufacturer will be listed on Tuesday, 29th October on the NSE SME platform. The shares will be allotted to bidders on October 25.

The company, established in 2017, has a fresh issue of up to 69,42,000 shares.

The company specialises in manufacturing a diverse range of high-quality engineered precision metal components for industries including automotive, defense, marine, telecom across 8 countries. The company’s product portfolio includes cut blanks, shafts, splines, torsion bars, piston rods, and more, catering to multiple sectors and regions.

With three manufacturing facilities located in Pune, Maharashtra, and one in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, OBSC Perfection has grown at over 26% CAGR over FY22 to FY24, the company said.

The company's business is dependent on the performance of the automotive industry. Economic cyclicality coupled with reduced demand in these other industries, in India or globally, could adversely affect the business, results of operations and financial condition.

The company proposes to utilize the net proceeds from the issue towards funding the capital expenditure requirements, working capital requirements and gerneral corporate purposes.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

According to multiple platforms that track the grey market premium activities, the shares are commanding an 11 percent GMP in the unofficial market.

India’s auto components industry’s market share has significantly expanded, led by increasing demand for automobiles by the growing middle class and exports globally.

The company aims to ride on the surging market as it caters to top original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) who ultimately supply various components and parts to top automotive manufacturing companies of India.

data.title: Deepak Builders & Engineers IPO bought over 32 times; check grey market premium and allotment date
data.description:
Deepak Builders & Engineers India IPO

Despite weakening sentiment across the equity markets, investors continued to show strong faith in Deepak Builders & Engineers India as its initial share sale received bids for 29.04 crore equity shares, 32.4 times higher against the offer size of 89.67 lakh shares on October 23, the last day of subscription. The benchmark Nifty shed seven percent from its record high of September 27.

The engineering and construction company targets to mop up Rs 260.04 crore through its initial public offering which is a combination of fresh issue of 1.07 crore shares worth Rs 217.21 crore, and an offer-for-sale of 21.1 lakh shares worth Rs 42.83 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Deepak Kumar Singal, and his wife Sunita Singal are the selling shareholders in the offer-for-sale. The price band for the issue, which was opened on October 21, is Rs 192-203 per share.

Non-institutional investors (high networth individuals) took the lead amongst them, buying 67.53 times the allotted quota, followed by retail investors who subscribed 33.01 times their reserved portion. Qualified institutional buyers also showed good interest in the issue, picking up nearly 5 times their portion set aside for them.

The Ludhiana-based company, which had mobilised Rs 78 crore from anchor investors on October 18, intends to spend Rs 142 crore out of net fresh issue proceeds for repaying its debt, and working capital requirements. And the remaining amount will be used for general corporate purposes.

Also read: Danish Power IPO GMP soars amid healthy subscription on day 2, indicating stellar listing gains

Deepak Builders & Engineers' IPO shares maintained healthy premium of 30 percent in the grey market over the upper price band ahead of debut next week, tracking strong subscription numbers, market observers said. The grey market is an unofficial market wherein IPO shares can be bought and sold till the listing on the bourses.

The company will finalise the basis of allotment of IPO shares by October 24, while the equity shares will be credited to demat accounts of successful investors by October 25.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Trading in its equity shares will commence on the BSE and NSE effective October 28.

data.title: Taking Stock: Market extend losses; Nifty below 24,450, Sensex down 139 pts
data.description:
The BSE Midcap index up 0.5 percent and Smallcap indices up 1 percent.

The final hour selling pressure erased all the intraday gains to end on a negative note in the volatile session on October 23 despite broader indices outperformance.

At close, the Sensex was down 138.74 points or 0.17 percent at 80,081.98, and the Nifty was down 36.60 points or 0.15 percent at 24,435.50.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

On the back of mixed global cues, the Indian indices opened on a negative note with Nifty below 24400, but buying at lower levels erased all the losses in the initial hour and traded in positive territory. However, final hour selling dragged the Nifty below 24,450.

Bajaj Finance, Bajaj Auto, Tech Mahindra, HCL Tech, Tata Consumer were among the top gainers on the Nifty, while losers included M&M, Sun Pharma, NTPC, Power Grid Corp and Shriram Finance.

The BSE Midcap index up 0.5 percent and Smallcap indices up 1 percent.

Among the sectors, IT index up more than 2 percent, while capital goods, power, pharma down 1 percent each.
